data.title: Retirement Savings at Risk Due to Market Volatility
data.description: The period surrounding retirement is a critical financial phase, where market downturns can significantly impact savings. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential risks retirees face if the market performs poorly during this time. Financial experts emphasize the importance of strategic planning to mitigate these risks and ensure financial stability in retirement. They suggest diversifying investments and maintaining a flexible withdrawal strategy to better navigate market fluctuations. As retirees rely on their savings for income, understanding and preparing for market volatility is crucial to safeguarding their financial future.

data.title: UK Prime Minister's Spokesperson: Too Early to Predict Iran Conflict's Impact on Energy Bills
data.description: The spokesperson for the UK Prime Minister, Will Wells, stated that it is premature to forecast the impact of the Iran conflict on energy bills. According to Jin10, Wells emphasized the uncertainty surrounding the situation and the difficulty in making accurate predictions at this stage. The ongoing geopolitical tensions have raised concerns about potential disruptions in energy supply, but the full extent of the impact remains unclear. The UK government continues to monitor the situation closely, assessing potential implications for the energy market and preparing to respond as necessary.

data.title: BlackRock's pledged Ethereum ETF surpasses $250 million in assets under management in its first week of trading.
data.description: BlackRock's iShares Staked Ethereum Trust (ETHB), a staked Ethereum exchange-traded fund, has reached $254 million in assets under management within a week of its listing. ETHB investors can receive 82% of their staking rewards through monthly dividends, with the remaining 18% distributed among the trust institutions, custodians, and service providers participating in the staking process. Currently, validators supporting the fund include Figment, Galaxy Blockchain Infrastructure, and Attestant. (Crypto-economy)

data.title: U.S. Restructures Foreign Aid Following USAID Closure
data.description: The Trump administration is undertaking a significant reorganization of foreign aid, reducing its scale considerably after the dismantling of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns from critics who argue that this decision has resulted in the loss of millions of lives. The restructuring aims to streamline operations and focus on more targeted assistance, but has faced backlash from humanitarian organizations and international partners. The administration's approach has sparked debate over the effectiveness and moral implications of reducing aid at a time when global needs are increasing. The changes reflect a broader shift in U.S. foreign policy priorities under President Donald Trump, emphasizing national interests over international commitments.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin is fluctuating around $70,000, derivatives are shifting towards defensive strategies, and the market is wary of sharp short-term volatility.
data.description: The crypto market is generally stabilizing, with BTC currently fluctuating around $70,500. However, derivatives data is showing clear defensive signals, and coupled with rising macroeconomic uncertainty, market sentiment remains under pressure. Derivatives data shows that Bitcoin open interest (OI) is stable at approximately $16.9 billion, funding rates have returned to the neutral range of 0%-10%, and the three-month annualized basis remains at 2.8%, reflecting a generally cautious institutional attitude. The options market exhibits a defensive structure, with the call/put ratio falling to 43/56, indicating a significant increase in downside hedging demand. Furthermore, short-term implied volatility has inverted, suggesting potential high-intensity volatility in the short term. In the past 24 hours, total liquidations across the network amounted to approximately $308 million, with long positions accounting for 63%. BTC and ETH saw liquidations of approximately $93 million and $81 million respectively. Binance's liquidation heatmap shows that $68,500 is a key liquidation support level that requires close monitoring. On the macro front, crude oil prices fell below the $100 mark to around $96. The possibility of the US considering releasing sanctioned Iranian oil to increase supply initially boosted risk assets, but the trend subsequently reversed, with US stock futures weakening. Nasdaq futures and S&P 500 futures fell by approximately 0.6% and 0.4% respectively, indicating that the market remains relatively fragile. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Ledger hires former Circle executive as CFO and prepares for IPO.
data.description: Crypto hardware wallet maker Ledger has hired former Circle executive John Andrews as its Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in preparation for its potential initial public offering (IPO). Andrews previously served as Head of Capital Markets and Investor Relations at stablecoin issuer Circle and was involved in its IPO. Ledger is also opening an office in New York to expand its U.S. operations. Andrews stated that the IPO plans may be delayed due to current market volatility. (The Information)

data.title: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) reports that the total market capitalization of virtual asset ETFs has exceeded HK$5.4 billion, representing a 142% increase since their launch.
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) released its Q4 2025 report, noting that it has officially become a member of the Hong Kong Police Force's Virtual Asset Intelligence Task Force. This strategic innovation has contributed to the continued growth of Hong Kong's listed and digital asset markets. Regarding digital assets, since its launch in 2025, the SFC-approved tokenized retail money market funds have seen their assets under management grow to HK$8.66 billion as of December last year, a 14% increase quarter-on-quarter. Furthermore, since the launch of Asian virtual asset spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) in 2024, 11 such ETFs have been listed in Hong Kong, with their total market capitalization increasing by 142% to over HK$5.4 billion since their inception. As of December last year, the total market capitalization of SFC-approved ETFs and leveraged and inverse products jumped 33.7% year-on-year to HK$618.7 billion. Furthermore, net capital inflows into funds registered in Hong Kong surged 118.5% year-on-year to HK$356.7 billion in 2025, while assets under management of these funds grew 38.3% year-on-year to HK$2.28 trillion as of December last year, and the total number of funds increased by 9.1% year-on-year to 1,041.

data.title: Experts Dismiss Money Laundering Concerns in Recent USDT to AAVE Swap
data.description: A recent transaction involving the conversion of $50 million in USDT to 327 AAVE, valued at approximately $37,000, has been deemed unlikely to be related to money laundering activities. According to NS3.AI, experts have suggested that the trade appears to be a user error rather than an illicit activity. The transaction was visible in Ethereum's public mempool and involved splitting the value across various participants and fees, further supporting the notion of a mistake rather than a deliberate attempt to launder money.

data.title: Surat Diamond Industry Faces Challenges Amid Economic Shifts
data.description: The Surat diamond industry is currently experiencing significant challenges due to various economic factors. Bloomberg posted on X that the industry, which is a major contributor to India's economy, is grappling with issues that are affecting its growth and stability. Industry experts are analyzing the situation to understand the underlying causes and potential solutions to these challenges. The diamond sector in Surat, known for its significant role in the global diamond market, is facing pressures that could have long-term implications for its operations and workforce. Stakeholders are closely monitoring the developments to assess the impact on the broader economic landscape.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Decline by 3%
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures experienced a decline of 3% today, currently trading at $3.069 per million British thermal units. According to Jin10, this drop reflects ongoing market volatility and fluctuating demand in the energy sector. Analysts are closely monitoring these movements as they assess the potential impact on broader energy markets.

data.title: Elle Fanning Stars in New Apple TV Series Exploring OnlyFans Dynamics
data.description: Elle Fanning takes on the role of a new mother who becomes an OnlyFans creator in David E. Kelley's latest Apple TV series, 'Margot’s Got Money Troubles.' Bloomberg posted on X, the series delves into the complexities of entrepreneurship, creativity, and the challenges faced on the platform.

data.title: Germany's February PPI Falls by 0.5%, Below Expectations
data.description: Germany's Producer Price Index (PPI) for February recorded a decline of 0.5%, according to Jin10. This figure fell short of the anticipated 0.30% increase and follows a previous decrease of 0.60%. The PPI measures the average change over time in the selling prices received by domestic producers for their output, and its decline may indicate potential deflationary pressures within the German economy. The unexpected drop in the PPI could influence economic forecasts and monetary policy decisions in the coming months.

data.title: Binance to Support Dai Token Swap and Rebranding to USDS
data.description: Binance has announced its support for the upcoming token swap and rebranding of Dai (DAI) to USDS (USDS). According to BWEnews, the centralized exchange (CEX) will facilitate the transition, ensuring a smooth process for its users. The rebranding initiative aims to enhance the token's market presence and align with strategic goals. Users are advised to stay informed about the changes and any potential impacts on their holdings.

data.title: Nvidia CEO Criticizes AI-Driven Layoffs, Highlights Growth Potential
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has criticized companies using artificial intelligence (AI) as a justification for layoffs, arguing that a lack of imagination is the real issue. In a conversation with Jim Cramer, Huang stated that while AI can enhance efficiency, companies with visionary leadership will leverage these tools to expand capabilities rather than reduce staff. He emphasized that firms struggling with innovation are merely using AI as an excuse for downsizing.

Huang pointed out the rapid revenue growth of AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, which reportedly see weekly increases of $10 to $20 billion. This challenges the narrative that AI companies are not profitable. Nvidia itself has seen a surge in orders, adding $500 billion in just five months, and continues to hire more employees.

Huang's comments suggest that the problem lies not with AI but with companies that have lacked new ideas for years and are now using AI as a convenient scapegoat.

data.title: AI TRENDS | PrismaX Joins NVIDIA's Inception Program
data.description:
PrismaX, a company specializing in Web3 embodied intelligent robots, has joined NVIDIA's Inception Program. According to Foresight News, this initiative allows PrismaX to access development tools, technical resources, training support, priority infrastructure access, and a broader ecosystem of builders and investors.

The Inception Program, launched by NVIDIA, is a free virtual accelerator designed to help startups in AI, data science, and high-performance computing (HPC) accelerate their growth.

data.title: South Korea Reviews Crypto Custody Provider After Security Breach
data.description: South Korea's National Tax Service is evaluating a private custody provider for seized cryptocurrencies following a security breach earlier this year. According to NS3.AI, the incident involved a leaked seed phrase in February, resulting in an unauthorized transfer of approximately $4.8 million in tokens. The agency is currently drafting criteria for selecting a provider and plans to finalize the choice by the first half of 2026. This review comes in the wake of another custody failure involving 22 BTC and a cross-agency investigation into the management of seized digital assets.

data.title: Perpetual DEX Trading Volumes Show Mixed Trends
data.description:
Recent data from DefiLlama indicates varied trends in trading volumes across several perpetual decentralized exchanges (Perp DEX). According to BlockBeats, while some platforms have experienced a decline in trading activity and a slight contraction in open interest, others have shown growth.

Hyperliquid has seen a rise in trading volume compared to the previous day, maintaining a high level of open interest. EdgeX, possibly driven by anticipation of its launch, remains active and holds the second position in trading volume.

The latest rankings for mainstream Perp DEX trading volumes are as follows:

Hyperliquid recorded a 24-hour trading volume of approximately $9.37 billion, with open contracts valued at around $7.0 billion.

EdgeX reported a 24-hour trading volume of about $3.54 billion, with open contracts totaling approximately $1.11 billion.

Aster's 24-hour trading volume was approximately $3.09 billion, with open contracts valued at around $1.86 billion.

Lighter had a 24-hour trading volume of about $1.85 billion, with open contracts totaling approximately $722 million.

Grvt recorded a 24-hour trading volume of approximately $1.54 billion, with open contracts valued at around $490 million.

Pacifica's 24-hour trading volume was about $441 million, with open contracts totaling approximately $75.81 million.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Spurs Oil Price Surge, Gas Supply Remains Stable
data.description: The recent outbreak of conflict in the Middle East has led to a sharp increase in oil prices. According to Ming Pao, Wong Wai-yee, Managing Director of Towngas (0003), stated that natural gas constitutes 60% of Towngas's supply. Due to a long-term 'take or pay' contract initiated in 2006 with Australian natural gas suppliers, Towngas is insulated from market price fluctuations. Since the implementation of this contract, the company has saved its customers over 20 billion HKD in fuel costs.

data.title: Binance Launches UP Trading Competition with $200K in Rewards
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X the launch of its UP Trading Competition, offering participants the chance to win rewards totaling $200,000. The competition invites traders to engage in buying activities, with only buy volume being considered for the reward calculation. Participants can track their trade volume by clicking 'Join' on the Binance App event page.

The competition is designed to encourage active trading among users, focusing solely on buy transactions. This approach aims to stimulate market activity and provide traders with an opportunity to earn substantial rewards. The exclusion of sell volume from the competition criteria underscores the emphasis on purchasing activity, aligning with Binance's strategy to enhance trading engagement.

Participants are urged to take advantage of this opportunity, as the rewards are significant and the competition is expected to attract a large number of traders. The event is structured to ensure fair play and transparency, with the tracking mechanism available directly through the Binance App. This initiative reflects Binance's ongoing commitment to fostering a dynamic trading environment and rewarding user participation.

data.title: Hunan prosecutors have cracked a money laundering case involving over 6.84 million yuan, which used transactions involving Moutai liquor as a cover for transferring funds through virtual currencies.
data.description: A money laundering case involving virtual currency, prosecuted by the Yueyang District People's Procuratorate of Xiangtan City, Hunan Province, has been adjudicated in the first instance. The case involved over 6.84 million yuan, and all eight defendants were sentenced. The case revealed that the gang concealed the flow of funds by fabricating transactions involving Moutai liquor, converting proceeds from telecommunications fraud into virtual currency to return to upstream suppliers, and charging a commission of approximately 8% per transaction. The individuals involved had a division of labor, including fund transfer, providing tools, and disguising transactions. The court sentenced seven defendants to prison terms ranging from two to six years for concealing and covering up the proceeds of crime, while one defendant received a suspended sentence. (Huasheng Online)
